page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6i-c8g1.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8163 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 31.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 3.88 GB per core. For acceleration, it includes a single NVIDIA T4 GPU based on the Turing architecture, offering 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. This instance does not include local storage and relies on network-attached storage, with a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. The Intel Xeon 8163 CPU delivers weak single-core performance, as evidenced by its Geekbench and stress-ng single-core scores. Multi-core performance is average, with a Passmark CPU score of 7041.46 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 5913.47. However, the integrated NVIDIA T4 GPU excels at machine learning workloads. LLM inference benchmarks show top-tier performance for prompt processing, achieving 8407.18 tokens per second on a small 135M model and 1134.43 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. Text generation speeds are also strong, reaching 252.92 tokens per second and 33.96 tokens per second for the 135M and 7B models, respectively.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.gn6i-c8g1.2xlarge offers a balanced entry point for GPU-accelerated computing without the high cost of top-tier enterprise accelerators. The tradeoff lies in its older CPU architecture and lack of local storage, which limits its effectiveness for high-throughput database operations or heavy CPU-bound compilation tasks. This instance is best suited for lightweight deep learning inference, small to medium LLM deployment, video transcoding, and entry-level graphics rendering workloads where GPU acceleration is required but massive CPU performance is not a priority.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6i-c8g1.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz, 31.0 GB of RAM, and a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. Built on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. While CPU benchmarks show weak single-core and average multi-core performance, the integrated Turing-architecture GPU delivers top-tier results in LLM inference prompt processing, reaching 1134.43 tokens per second on 7B models. This hardware profile offers a cost-effective solution for GPU-dependent workloads, trading off raw CPU speed and local storage for affordable acceleration. It is highly suited for small-to-medium machine learning inference, model deployment, and video processing tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6i-c8g1.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 31 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4 GPU. It delivers strong LLM inference performance for entry-level AI workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6i-c8g1.2xlarge features 8 vCPUs, 31 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4 GPU, delivering strong performance for machine learning inference.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring an NVIDIA T4 GPU for efficient machine learning inference and entry-level parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8163 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 31.0 GB RAM
NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB VRAM for hardware-accelerated workloads
Top-tier prompt processing performance for small and medium LLM inference
No local storage, relying on network-attached storage options
Best suited for machine learning inference, video transcoding, and parallel processing
